title: hieracium tenuiflorum subsp. sebini
intro: Hieracium tenuiflorum subsp. sebini, also known as Mountain Hawkweed or Hieracium sebium, is a plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Hieracium tenuiflorum subsp. sebini include its ability to reduce fever, relieve pain, and treat digestive issues, as well as its potential to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
